assertions.says: Saving an artifact that was committed outside the pipeline still records its metadata
Saving an artifact that was committed outside the pipeline captures which files were changed
Saving an artifact that was committed outside the pipeline produces a commit
Saving the same artifact twice without changes does not create a spurious commit
Metadata write is skipped when the artifact hash has not changed
Metadata timestamp is preserved when the artifact hash has not changed
Metadata write preserves existing entries like pre-check and modules_touched
Artifact save succeeds when run from a subdirectory
Work complete succeeds when run from a subdirectory
Work complete blocks when build-report was never saved through the pipeline
Work complete blocks when verify-report was never saved through the pipeline
Work complete blocks when both reports are missing and names both
Work complete blocks when both reports are missing and names the verify-report too
Work complete proceeds when both reports have valid save metadata
The completeness check does not mutate any files when it fails
A failed work complete is automatically recovered on retry
Recovery commits the archived plan and proof chain
A genuinely completed work item is reported as already completed
Double recovery works when the first recovery also fails
Main-path commit failure tells the user how to retry
